channel.title: AUA AI Hub Weekly Digest
channel.description: Weekly digest of artificial intelligence news for the AUA College of Medicine community.

item.title: AUA AI Hub Digest: Week of June 8, 2026
item.description:
Medical Education

  • The role of emerging technology in expanding regional anesthesia education and patient access (PubMed AI in medical education). Emerging technologies are identified as enhancing regional anesthesia education and expanding patient access.
  • Self-learning and Simulation in the 21st Century: From Textbooks to ChatGPT (PubMed AI in medical education). The role of digital tools including ChatGPT in self-learning and simulation during medical training is examined.
  • Explainable Machine Learning Integrating Patient and Environmental Factors for Predicting Multidrug-Resistant Organism Colonization or Infection on ICU Admission (PubMed AI in medical education). Machine learning integrating patient and environmental factors predicted multidrug-resistant organism colonization and infection.
  • Application of an artificial-intelligence-based transesophageal echocardiography simulation system in residency training (PubMed AI in medical education). An artificial intelligence-based transesophageal echocardiography simulation system improved resident training performance.
  • Evaluating a DeepSeek-Based Case-Based Learning Approach for Teaching the Emergency Management of Urosepsis to Nursing Students (PubMed AI in medical education). A DeepSeek-based case-based learning approach improved nursing students' emergency management education for urosepsis.
  • Integrating Artificial Intelligence-Based Simulation into Serious Illness Conversation Training: A Pilot Medical Student Intervention (PubMed AI in medical education). A pilot study tested artificial intelligence-based simulation as a training tool for medical students to practice serious illness conversations.
  • Large language models in emergency and critical care medicine: a comprehensive review of applications, challenges, and future directions (PubMed AI in medical education). Emergency and critical care medicine requires the rapid synthesis of heterogeneous clinical data under extreme time constraints.
  • Development and clinical usability assessment of a web-based AI platform for automated echocardiography analysis (PubMed AI in medical education). A web-based AI platform, EchoAI, showed vendor-agnostic, domain-adaptive automated echocardiography analysis with clinical usability.
  • Re-Centering Clinical Documentation in the Age of AI Scribes: Four Aims of the Patient Chart Note (JMIR Medical Education). Clinical documentation is a foundational skill in medicine, developed during training and required in everyday practice.
  • Perspectives of Premedical Students at a Medical University College in Malaysia Regarding Augmented Reality Integration in Anatomy Education: Cross-Sectional Study (JMIR Medical Education). Background: For novice anatomy learners, studying human anatomy using textbooks, 2D learning materials, and static anatomical models frequently causes challenges in understanding complex anatomical structures.
  • AI Integration in Spanish Undergraduate Medical Education: National Cross-Sectional Study (JMIR Medical Education). Background: Artificial intelligence (AI) is reshaping clinical practice and redefining the competencies future physicians will need.
  • 360 Video-Based Virtual Reality for Preparing Medical Students for Body Donor Dissection: Randomized Controlled Trial (PubMed AI in medical education). CONCLUSIONS: The 360° video-based VR intervention significantly reduced TA and was perceived as valuable for emotional and procedural preparation.
  • Narrative Review on History of Point-of-Care Ultrasound Training in Critical Care (PubMed AI in medical education). CONCLUSION: PoCUS training has advanced considerably but remains fragmented.
  • Artificial intelligence in clinical physiology: System-wise applications in diagnostics, monitoring, and medical education (PubMed AI in medical education). CONCLUSION: AI possesses the potential to revolutionize clinical physiology by facilitating predictive, personalized, and participatory healthcare.
  • Knowledge, Attitude, and Perception of Medical Students Toward AI-Based Learning: A Cross-Sectional Study (PubMed AI in medical education). CONCLUSION: Medical students in KP exhibit strong enthusiasm and positive perceptions toward AI-based learning, yet their knowledge remains modest and uneven across training levels.
  • Integrating AI into undergraduate medical education: an exploration of learner-centered approaches through AI (PubMed AI in medical education). INTRODUCTION: Advances in artificial intelligence (AI) offer promising opportunities to enrich medical education by supporting self-directed learning, providing personalized feedback, and simulating clinical and scientific reasoning.
  • Deep learning-based assessment of periodontitis progression using serial panoramic radiographs: a multicenter study incorporating indirect and direct grading approaches (PubMed AI in medical education). CONCLUSIONS: This multicenter study demonstrates that DL-based assessment of periodontitis progression using serial panoramic radiographs achieves high diagnostic accuracy (89.6% and 79.5% for indirect and direct assessment, respectively) while markedly improving time efficiency.
  • Reconciling how clinical reasoning is learned in the age of artificial intelligence (PubMed AI in medical education). Recent studies explore how artificial intelligence supports medical students' learning of clinical reasoning.
  • EXPRESS: Effect of the Use of Artificial Intelligence Tools on Clinical Education and Clinical Decision-Making of Medical Students During Clinical Rotations: A Scoping Review (PubMed AI in medical education). Medical education is transforming due to new technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI).
  • Adoption and Use of Social Media in Health Care Among Medical Residents: Cross-Sectional Study (JMIR Medical Education). Background: Social media apps are widely used by health care professionals despite security and regulatory risks.
  • Development and Validation of a Scale for Measuring Leadership and Managerial Competencies of Middle Managers in Health Care and Medical Education in the Gulf Region: Cross-Sectional Study (JMIR Medical Education). Background: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the Kingdom of Bahrain are transforming their health care systems toward more self-sustained, autonomous systems.
  • Physical Attractiveness and Chances of Being Invited to Interview With a Medical Residency Program: Retrospective Cohort Study (JMIR Medical Education). Background: Applicants participating in the Residency Match generally submit a photograph through the Electronic Residency Application Service (ERAS).
  • AI-Assisted Formative Assessment in Clinical Education: From Algorithms to Agency (JMIR Medical Education). Artificial intelligence (AI) is rapidly reshaping clinical education by embedding assessment and feedback into everyday learning activities.
  • From Classroom to Operating Room: Addressing Shortcomings in Gender Affirmation Surgical Education (JMIR Medical Education). The prevalence of American youth identifying as transgender has doubled in the past five years, emphasizing the crucial role of gender affirmation surgery (GAS) in treating gender dysphoria.

Clinical Practice

  • Understanding Transformer-Based Classifications of Medical Text Using a Large Language Model for the Attribution of Feature Importance: Proof-of-Concept Algorithm Development and Validation Study (JMIR Medical Informatics). This study investigates transformer-based deep learning models for classifying medical text and explores feature importance attribution using a large language model.
  • Development and application of an intelligent assessment system for medical clinical skill training (npj Digital Medicine). An intelligent assessment system was developed and applied for medical clinical skill training.
  • Smartphone-based detection of subtle memory decline in prodromal Alzheimer’s disease (npj Digital Medicine). Smartphone-based detection methods identified subtle memory decline in early Alzheimer's disease.
  • Human, AI collaboration for dysphagia rehabilitation from effectiveness to implementation complexity: a systematic review (npj Digital Medicine). A systematic review examined human and artificial intelligence collaboration for dysphagia rehabilitation including effectiveness and implementation challenges.
  • Brain Health for Economic Resilience: a data-driven framework for the brain-positive economic transition (Nature Medicine). A data-driven framework was introduced linking brain health to economic resilience for brain-positive economic transition.
  • Dual-target gene therapy in Parkinson’s disease: a multicenter phase 1 trial (Nature Medicine). Phase 1 trial results were reported for dual-target gene therapy in Parkinson's disease.
  • Noninvasive PPROM risk stratification with explainable AI using routine antenatal CRP and albumin (npj Digital Medicine). The study presents an explainable artificial intelligence model for noninvasive risk stratification of preterm premature rupture of membranes based on routine antenatal C-reactive protein and albumin measurements.
  • Benchmarking deep learning architectures for MALDI-TOF mass spectrometry in infectious disease diagnostics and vector-borne disease surveillance (npj Digital Medicine). This paper benchmarks deep learning architectures for matrix-assisted laser desorption/ionization time-of-flight mass spectrometry applied to infectious disease diagnostics and vector-borne disease surveillance.
  • Predicting 5-Year Mortality in Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer Using the Korean Central Cancer Registry: Model Development and Validation Study (JMIR Medical Informatics). Background: Non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC) is one of the most common cancers and a leading cause of cancer-related mortality, making prognostic prediction clinically essential.
  • Federated orthogonal learning for detection of liver lesions from multi-phase contrast-enhanced CT images (npj Digital Medicine). Researchers present a federated orthogonal learning method for detecting liver lesions using multi-phase contrast-enhanced computed tomography images without sharing patient data across centers.
  • Knowledge, Attitudes, Practices, Barriers, and Promotional Strategies Related to Clinical Data Interchange Standards Consortium Adoption Among Clinical Data Management Professionals: Semiqualitative Interview Study (JMIR Medical Informatics). Background: National Medical Products Administration of China has actively encouraged organizations to adopt the Clinical Data Interchange Standards Consortium (CDISC) for clinical data submission since 2020.
  • Histopathological Assessment of Myocardial Ischemia-Reperfusion Injury Using Transformer-Based Artificial Intelligence: Model Comparison Study (JMIR Medical Informatics). Background: Myocardial ischemia-reperfusion injury (MIRI) poses diagnostic challenges due to complex histopathological changes.
  • Early Immunological Biomarkers for Personalized Treatment Selection in Severe COVID-19: Post Hoc Machine Learning Analysis of a Randomized Clinical Trial (JMIR Medical Informatics). Background: Severe COVID-19 is a global health concern despite continuous vaccination campaigns because current therapies, such as dexamethasone and remdesivir, do not considerably improve immune function, especially in high-risk individuals.
  • Advancing Gastrointestinal Cancer Risk Prediction With Patient-Centered Machine Learning: Machine Learning Modeling Study (JMIR Medical Informatics). Background: Gastrointestinal (GI) cancers are a significant health concern in South Korea.
  • Clinical Note Generation From Doctor-Patient Conversations Using Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning Large Language Models: Comparative Study (JMIR Medical Informatics). Background: Clinical note documentation is a vital yet time-intensive task in health care.
  • Development and Interpretability Analysis of a Stacking Ensemble Model for Early Prediction of Nutritional Risk in Intensive Care Unit Patients: Retrospective Cohort Study (JMIR Medical Informatics). Background: Malnutrition in critically ill patients is associated with increased morbidity and mortality, yet traditional screening tools such as the modified NUTRIC (mNUTRIC) score often rely on subjective assessments or delayed data, limiting their utility for early intervention in the dynamic intensive care unit (ICU) environment.
  • Novel Online Platform for Trauma Care-Integrating Trauma Phenotypes to Optimize the Trauma and Injury Severity Score Model: Retrospective Cohort Study (JMIR Medical Informatics). Background: Severe trauma remains a leading cause of admission to the intensive care unit.
  • Improving Vancomycin Therapeutic Drug Monitoring With a Deep Learning, Based Two-Compartment Predictive Model: Development and Validation Study (JMIR AI). Researchers developed and validated a deep learning two-compartment pharmacokinetic recurrent neural network model to improve vancomycin therapeutic drug monitoring.
  • Estimating a Physiological Lung Function Score and Biological Sex Using Pulmonary Function Tests and Machine Learning: Retrospective Study (JMIR AI). A machine learning model was trained to estimate physiological lung function scores and biological sex using pulmonary function tests for better understanding of sex- and age-related differences.
  • The Role of Multimodal Generative AI in Older Adults’ Health Management: Systematic Scoping Review (JMIR AI). A systematic scoping review examined the role of multimodal generative artificial intelligence in managing health for older adults amid global aging challenges.
  • Multimodal GPT-5 for Predicting Poor Functional Outcomes After Intracerebral Hemorrhage in the Emergency Department: Validation Study (JMIR AI). A study validated a multimodal GPT-5 model to predict poor functional outcomes after intracerebral hemorrhage in the emergency department to aid early clinical decisions.

General AI

  • Initial impressions of Claude Fable 5 (Simon Willison's weblog). I didn't have early access to today's Claude Fable 5 release, but I've spent the past ~5.5 hours putting it through its paces.
  • Can Voice Agents Handle Bilingual Customers? Benchmarking Frontier ASR on Code-Switched Speech (Hugging Face blog).
  • OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS (Hacker News (LLM, 100+ points)). OpenAI's frontier models and Codex code generation models are now available for use on Amazon Web Services cloud platform.
  • CS336: Language Modeling from Scratch (Hacker News (LLM, 100+ points)). Stanford's CS336 course provides an educational resource on language modeling from scratch, useful for understanding foundational AI concepts.

Videos

  • Introducing Claude Fable 5 (Anthropic)
  • Anthropic Calls for "Global AI Pause" (Wes Roth)
  • DeepMind’s New AI Found A Strange New Way To Think (Two Minute Papers)
  • OpenClaw Security Risks: 6 Dangers of Autonomous AI Agents (IBM Technology)
  • Claude Opus 4.8: Lying Machine No More? (Two Minute Papers)
  • What Happens After A 1,000,000x AI Compute Leap? | Jeff Dean (Two Minute Papers)
  • Why AI Models Pause to Think: Test Time Compute Explained (IBM Technology)
  • MiniMax M3 IS INSANE! BEST Opensource AI Model! Beats Opus 4.7 and 50x Cheaper! (Fully Tested) (WorldofAI)
  • Why One Law School Just Banned AI (AI in Education Podcast)
  • Breaking down the 2026 Stanford AI Index Report (Practical AI)
  • AI for Interoperability in Health Care: Philips’s Carla Goulart Peron (Me, Myself, and AI)
